title: alpinia minuta
intro: Alpinia minuta, also known as the lesser galangal or Thai ginger, is a species of flowering plant in the ginger family (Zingiberaceae) native to Southeast Asia. Alpinia minuta has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Alpinia minuta include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate pain, making it a popular ingredient in traditional remedies for various ailments. Alpinia minuta can be prepared as a tea, powder, or oil, and is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
